cve_remediation_workflow.overview: This workflow defines automated CVE remediation for Bob Shell running in GitHub Actions. It is the shell-oriented counterpart to the interactive DevOps workflow. GitHub MCP is the preferred GitHub integration path, while local git performs workspace changes and `gh` or authenticated REST API act only as fallback for failed MCP operations.

workflow_context.trigger_source: GitHub Actions workflow prompt or issue-triggered automation
workflow_context.approval_model: Bob completes the package remediation phase and exits. Human review and approval happen in GitHub after the PR is created. Bob does not wait inside the shell session for approval events.

phase.name: resolve_issue_context
phase.title: Resolve the GitHub issue and remediation target

step.action: Use GitHub MCP first to fetch the target issue details

accepted_inputs.input: Explicit issue URL in the prompt
Explicit issue number in the prompt
Repository plus issue number from workflow context

step.action: Extract CVE ID, package name, repository, and application context from the issue body and title
If GitHub MCP fails, use `gh` CLI or authenticated REST API as fallback and record that fallback was used

phase.output: Repository owner and repo, issue number, CVE ID, package name, and any relevant severity or application context

phase.name: research_concert_and_public_sources
phase.title: Research with Concert first, then validate externally

prerequisites.prerequisite: Concert credentials are available from environment variables or `.env`

steps.step: Load Concert credentials from `.env` or the shell environment
Query Concert to identify the affected application and recommended package version
Cross-reference the CVE with at least one authoritative public source such as NVD or GitHub Advisory data
Choose the remediation version using Concert as the primary internal source of truth, while documenting any mismatch with public advisories

phase.output: Confirmed remediation version, risk context, and justification

phase.name: create_and_checkout_branch
phase.title: Create remediation branch before modifying files

rules.rule: Never modify files on main or master
Create the branch before editing any dependency manifest
Prefer GitHub MCP create_branch first, then checkout locally

steps.step: Attempt remote branch creation with GitHub MCP
If the branch already exists or MCP branch creation fails, use local git checkout or local branch creation as fallback
Verify the current branch before modifying files

scope_rules.rule: Only modify dependency manifests, lockfiles, or package metadata required for the CVE fix
Do not modify application source files
Do not perform code compatibility remediation in this mode

steps.step: Keep changes focused, minimal, and attributable to the CVE fix

phase.name: commit_push_and_pr_creation
phase.title: Commit, push, and create the pull request

steps.step: Commit the manifest changes using a structured CVE remediation message
Push the remediation branch to origin
Use GitHub MCP create_pull_request as the preferred PR creation path
If GitHub MCP PR creation fails, use `gh` CLI or authenticated REST API as fallback
Optionally add labels, reviewers, and comments with GitHub MCP first and fallback only when necessary

phase.name: completion_output
phase.title: Emit parseable completion output
phase.requirement: The final shell output must be stable and parseable by the GitHub Actions workflow.

out_of_scope.item: Waiting for approval inside Bob Shell
Running tests as part of this mode’s success criteria
Fixing broken application code after package upgrade
Updating test code to restore compatibility
